title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Automation and Data Client Syste… · Behavior Case: Timeout Layers
summary: A nightly reconciliation has a fixed run window and must divide deadlines across resolution, connect, headers, streamed body, parsing, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py timeout layers increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
